sections.paragraph:
φραγελλόω (φραγγέλιον; in Christian usage [s. end of this entry]; but cp. TestBenj 2:3 and Aesop fr. the Cod. Paris. 1277: CRochefort, Notices et Extraits II [1789] 719 no. 19) 1 aor. ἐφραγέλλωσα (Lat. loanw.: flagello; s. φραγέλλιον) flog, scourge, a punishment inflicted on slaves and provincials after a sentence of death had been pronounced on them. So in the case of Jesus before the crucifixion (cp. Jos., Bell. 2, 306 οὓς μάστιξιν προαικισάμενος ἀνεσταύρωσεν [sc. Φλῶρος]; 5, 449; Lucian, Pisc. 2) Mt 27:26; Mk 15:15 (Mommsen, Röm. Strafrecht 1899, 938f; 983f; RAC IX, 469–90; contrast the action Lk 23:16 παιδεύω; J 19:1 μαστιγόω). Performed after sentence to animal combat AcPl Ha 1, 30.—RGundry, Mark ’93, 938f (reff.). DELG s.v. φραγέλλιον.

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

TestBenj TestBenj = Testament of Benjamin, s. Test12Patr—List 2

Aesop Aesop = a calque for a variety of lit. associated with the name of Aesop—List 5

  1. fr. = from

aor. aor. = aorist

Lat. Lat. = Latin

loanw. loanw. = loanword

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

  1. sc. = scilicet (one may understand, supply)

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

RAC RAC = Reallexikon für Antike u. Christentum—List 6

AcPl Ha AcPl Ha = Acts of Paul, PHamb—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 1064
